decisions.summary: Implement the entire stylometry engine patch plan as a single consolidated patch, not a phased rollout.
decisions.reasoning: User was instructed to deliver one patch, upload to GitHub, and hand off. Splitting comes later for personal use.

decisions.summary: Competitive candidacy architecture: beam search candidates compete with legacy candidates; best score wins.
decisions.reasoning: Replacing the legacy pipeline entirely was too risky. Competitive approach means beam search is additive — if it produces garbage, legacy path is the safety net.

decisions.summary: Hard pathology filters (binary fail, not soft penalties) for banned connector stacks, orphan fragments, and additive collapse.
decisions.reasoning: A stylometry engine that makes text worse is worse than useless. Engine returns source text unchanged rather than emit pathological output.

decisions.summary: Transfer classes must be honest: weak/rejected are correct answers, not failures to fix.
decisions.reasoning: Short or rigid texts genuinely lack structural rewrite opportunity. False 'structural' classifications are a worse outcome than honest 'weak'.

decisions.summary: Fix pre-existing bugs (contraction reversal, double periods, merge-undo, pass decomposition) as part of the patch.
decisions.reasoning: New architecture could not be tested until these bugs were fixed. They existed in the original codebase and failed in the original test suite.

decisions.summary: Acceptance thresholds proposed by AI and accepted by user (stylometry not user's strong point).
decisions.reasoning: User explicitly delegated threshold design. Thresholds are encoded in benchmark.test.mjs and can be tuned by the stylometry reviewer.

decisions.summary: Handoff document written in dry humor / bitchy professional tone per user request.
decisions.reasoning: User's exact words: 'can you make it a bitchy, dry humor soaked document' with caveat 'I am a professional and allowed to bitch.' Company culture.

artifacts.name: stylometry.js
artifacts.description: Core stylometry engine — all new IR, operator, beam search, and pathology systems live here.
artifacts.changes_summary: Added IR system (segmentTextToIR, clause hierarchy), 15 deterministic operators (4 families), 8-strategy beam search, pathology detection, 4 bug fixes (contraction reversal, double periods, merge-undo, pass decomposition), enhanced debug output. ~3278 lines.

artifacts.name: browser-engine.js
artifacts.description: Browser bundle mirror of stylometry.js. Must be kept in sync manually.
artifacts.changes_summary: Full mirror of all stylometry.js changes. ~3635 lines.

artifacts.name: browser-main.js
artifacts.description: UI + test flight. 14 total transfer test-flight cases.
artifacts.changes_summary: Added 7 new test-flight cases: screenshot pairs (ref→probe, probe→ref), compression/expansion structural, additive collapse blocked, connector stacking blocked. ~2862 lines.

artifacts.name: benchmark.test.mjs
artifacts.description: Acceptance test suite: 13 test cases, 43 assertions across screenshot pairs, structural contrast, low-opportunity, pathology detection, and acceptance thresholds.
artifacts.changes_summary: New file. Sections: A (screenshot pairs), B (structural contrast 4-way), C (low-opportunity), D (pathology detection), plus acceptance criteria validators. 385 lines.

artifacts.name: stylometry.test.mjs
artifacts.description: Original test suite. Unchanged, still passes.
artifacts.changes_summary: No changes. 298 lines, 30+ assertions, all passing.

artifacts.name: HANDOFF.md
artifacts.description: Developer handoff document for the stylometry reviewer. Dry humor tone. Covers all changes, architecture decisions, verification steps, and known risks.

code_changes.scope: IR system (segmentTextToIR, segmentSentenceToClauses, classifyClauseType, detectClauseCompleteness, detectModalityAndHedges, buildOpportunityProfileFromIR)
code_changes.summary: Clause-level intermediate representation that parses source text into SentenceNode → ClauseNode hierarchy. Foundation for beam search and operator library.
Stateless text-rewriting operators: compression (6), expansion (6), connector-family (1), hedge/stance (3). Each takes text + IR + target profile, returns transformed text.
Runs 8 parallel strategies over operator library. Weighted scoring considers cadence distance, pathology count, text preservation. Competes with legacy candidates.
Hard filters for banned connector stacks (though if, honestly; and, but because), orphan fragments, additive collapse. Binary fail in quality gate.
Baseline floor applied contractions, then later pass reversed them because target density < current density after contractions added. Fixed with mod-direction override. Pre-existing bug.
Added .replace(/\.{2,}/g, '.') to clean double periods created by multiple splitting passes. Pre-existing bug.
applyPunctuationTexture was converting merge-join semicolons back to periods. Guarded with !(transferPlan.wantsLonger && (candidateMod.punc || 0) < 0). Pre-existing bug.
Decomposed monolithic baseline pass into 7 separate runPass calls (merge/split, contraction, phrase, discourse, stance, function-word, line-break). Required for passesApplied.length >= 2 assertion.

blockers.description: Pre-existing test failures in original codebase (contraction reversal, passesApplied count) blocked new feature testing.
blockers.resolution: Verified failures existed on original zip, then fixed root causes (mod-direction override, pass decomposition).

blockers.description: Banned connector stacks ('honestly; and') appearing in legacy candidate output.
blockers.resolution: Added BANNED_CONNECTOR_STACKS regex array and orphan fragment detection to evaluateTransferQuality.

blockers.description: browser-engine.js must be manually mirrored from stylometry.js — no automated sync.
blockers.resolution: Manual mirror completed. Documented as known risk in HANDOFF.md.

open_questions.question: Are the acceptance thresholds (structural/weak/rejected classification boundaries) tuned correctly for the stylometry reviewer's use cases?
open_questions.context: Thresholds were proposed by AI, accepted by user who self-identifies as not a stylometry expert. The stylometry reviewer should validate.

open_questions.question: Should the browser-engine.js mirror be replaced with a build step or module bundler?
open_questions.context: Manual mirroring is error-prone and the file is 3600+ lines. Currently no build toolchain exists in the project.

open_questions.question: Will the beam search operator library need expansion for edge cases the reviewer encounters?
open_questions.context: 15 operators cover the major structural patterns in English. Unusual sentence constructions may need new operators.

dependencies.blocked_item: Tawanna's BFF's neural net integration (masking mirroring patterns)
dependencies.depends_on: Stylometry reviewer validates and accepts the patch

energy_arc.notes: Session maintained high throughput throughout. Multiple parallel agents used effectively. User delegated confidently and let the work proceed without micromanaging.

drift_events.trigger: Discovery that pre-existing bugs needed fixing before new architecture could be tested.
drift_events.notes: Not really drift — necessary foundation work. But it wasn't in the original plan.

load_signals.notes: User noted stylometry is not their strong point and deferred threshold decisions. Appropriate delegation, not disengagement.

emergence.idea: Competitive candidacy architecture (beam + legacy, best wins) as a safe deployment strategy.
All 4 bug fixes were pre-existing in the original codebase — nobody had run the tests.

unspoken_tensions.area: The manual browser-engine.js mirror is a maintenance liability that will eventually bite someone.
unspoken_tensions.notes: Documented in HANDOFF.md. Not this session's problem to solve, but it's lurking.

next_actions.action: Stylometry reviewer validates patch against their own test corpus and use cases.
Upload patch to GitHub (all modified files + new benchmark.test.mjs + HANDOFF.md).
Wait for bounce-back feedback from stylometry reviewer.
If accepted: begin neural net integration work using the stylometry engine output.

continuity.context_packet: Single consolidated patch to TCP stylometry engine implementing deterministic cadence transduction. Core additions: IR system (clause-level parse), 15-operator library (compression/expansion/connector/hedge families), 8-strategy beam search competing with legacy candidates, hard pathology filters (banned connector stacks, orphan fragments, additive collapse). Four pre-existing bugs fixed (contraction reversal, double periods, merge-undo, pass decomposition). All changes mirrored to browser-engine.js. New benchmark test suite: 13 cases, 43 assertions, all passing. Original test suite unchanged and passing. 7 new browser test-flight cases (14 total). HANDOFF.md created for stylometry reviewer. Patch handed off 2026-03-20. Tawanna's BFF waiting for bounce-back. Neural net integration (masking mirroring patterns) depends on patch acceptance.

risk_flags.risk: Acceptance thresholds were set by AI, not domain expert. Stylometry reviewer may disagree.
risk_flags.mitigation: Thresholds are in benchmark.test.mjs and easily adjustable. Document says 'do not loosen' but reviewer has final authority.

risk_flags.risk: browser-engine.js manual mirror can silently drift from stylometry.js if future changes aren't mirrored.
risk_flags.mitigation: Documented prominently in HANDOFF.md. Consider adding a build step long-term.

risk_flags.risk: Beam search operators use hardcoded English regex patterns. Non-English text will not benefit.
risk_flags.mitigation: Out of scope for this patch. Flag if multilingual support becomes a requirement.

suggested_next_session.focus_area: Respond to stylometry reviewer feedback, if any. Otherwise, neural net integration.
suggested_next_session.energy_note: This session was sustained and productive. Next session can be lighter — just responding to feedback unless there's a major rework.
suggested_next_session.warm_up: Re-run both test suites to confirm nothing has drifted, then read reviewer's notes.
